title: Q related Article Archive
wikitext:
[]: Q-related Article Archive             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Oct 18, 2012 3:50 pm Seems like such a thread would be useful. Think of it as the cross between Rebuild scans and CRC threads, full of hopefully useful and quotable info, all neatly organized. Thoughts? Only problem so far is we really haven't had any useful traditional magazine articles yet. Q cometh November 17th. The last batch of hobby magazines (Dengeki Hobby, Hobby Japan, etc) before that is October 25th. Nothing happened. The last batch of anime magazines (Newtype, Animage, etc.) before that is November 10th. Subtract 3-5 days from street date for crappy cell phone pic leaks. Nothing announced by the published yet, but hopefully we'll get something then. -----------------==============Article Archive==============----------------- 2011 June Newtype - Studio Khara feature article , contained no actual Q news. Another relevant thread 2012 February Newtype - EVA:02'β P.M. image and info that was announced New Year's Day 2012 July 14th Front Runner - Hideaki Anno Interview

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 18, 2012 3:59 pm Sounds useful, yeah. Stickied! I remember I kind of assumed November Newtype Japan (released on 10/10 this month) would have had Eva Q news but there was nothing. (I think.) I guess December Newtype could have some kind of cover feature about Q?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:01 pm Japanese Interview with Hiroyuki Okiura, he seemingly worked on the original picture design (whatever that means) for Q: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-1849.html Although, I don't notice his name in the staff information here , unless he doesn't have a lead staff credit.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 27, 2012 7:25 pm "Original picture" is genga , better translated to "key frame animation". I have no idea what 模様 would translate to in this instance.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 27, 2012 9:44 pm He isn't big name staff, but he's a damn awesome animator that has lots of great work under his belt. http://www18.atwiki.jp/sakuga/pages/8.html His contribution should be quite noticible.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 27, 2012 9:59 pm He's worked on both Ghost in the shell and Cowboy Bebop it's a definite plus.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Oct 27, 2012 10:01 pm youtube seems to have nuked a lot have a sample of his work http://www.nicovideo.jp/watch/sm2324 For those with no nico accounts: http://nicoviewer.net/sm2324

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 27, 2012 10:10 pm So many of my favorite anime in that mix, I can't wait to see what he does in 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by Knuckles » Sun Oct 28, 2012 2:04 pm that clip show is impressive. definitely great news for Eva 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Nov 10, 2012 1:30 am Well the last batch of magazines before theatrical release is out and none of them seem to have anything at all Eva related. What the hell are they planning that they're not revealing anything in advance?

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Sat Nov 10, 2012 1:42 am ^You can never have enough money.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 10, 2012 11:18 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: What the hell are they planning that they're not revealing anything in advance?

[]: Revered man returns...             Quote Post by XX » Mon Nov 12, 2012 11:36 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: He isn't big name staff, but he's a damn awesome animator that has lots of great work under his belt. http://www18.atwiki.jp/sakuga/pages/8.html His contribution should be quite noticible.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 12, 2012 11:41 am Hiroyuki Okiura is legendary tier animator with instantly recognizable touch. Him being involved with 3.0 actually IS a big deal. Hiroyuki Okiura animation in my Eva? oh god YES YES YES

[]: Re: Revered man returns...             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:02 pm XX wrote: He is actually a really. BIG. name ... he is a legend!

[]: Re: Revered man returns...             Quote Post by XX » Mon Nov 12, 2012 1:11 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: What I meant by "He isn't big name staff" was a response to this:

[]: Re: Q-related Article Archive             Quote Post by dummysystem » Sun Nov 25, 2012 8:33 pm maybe not only related to 3.0 but to the whole Rebuild : booklet of 1.11 (I'm sure you can read it directly form your 1.11 copy) I find this pretty interesting, as it echoes the different theories about 3.0. I think that we're actually reviving episode #25-26 from the TV ( a world that could have existed) . If you remember '96, there was a lot of rage against Anno because of these 2 episodes ( the result of this was Death & Rebirth). I remember reading somewhere that through eva, Anno wanted to make a critic of the Japanese animation/otaku, stuff like think by yourself. In the booklet, Anno also says : [...]I feel that there needs to be a work of animation targeted towards them

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 28, 2012 10:12 am Pastebin links to theatrical booklet interviews being translated by /A/nonymous: http://pastebin.com/Ygr32fm0

[]:             Quote Post by dummysystem » Thu Nov 29, 2012 8:41 am not sure if this is the correct thread. Kotaku has an article describing some analysis of the 6mn trailer, in term of mechanics ( how eva02 is making the rendezvous with eva01 and some other description about space orbits, etc ...) The analysis has been done by the Tokyo Metropolitan University Space Systems Laboratory. Kotaku post : [url]http://kotaku.com/5964200/heres-why-the-new-evangelion-movie-is-surprisingly-realistic?[/url] Space Systems Laboratory article : [url]http://www.sd.tmu.ac.jp/ssl/discussion_q.html[/url] Still interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Thu Nov 29, 2012 11:45 am dummysystem wrote: not sure if this is the correct thread. Kotaku has an article describing some analysis of the 6mn trailer, in term of mechanics ( how eva02 is making the rendezvous with eva01 and some other description about space orbits, etc ...) The analysis has been done by the Tokyo Metropolitan University Space Systems Laboratory. Kotaku post : [url]http://kotaku.com/5964200/heres-why-the-new-evangelion-movie-is-surprisingly-realistic?[/url] Space Systems Laboratory article : [url]http://www.sd.tmu.ac.jp/ssl/discussion_q.html[/url] Still interesting.
